message:
test: conformance coverage buckets + compat_tests ancillary-flag parity + shm SAFETY doc

crates/fsqlite-core/tests/conformance_oracle_ext.rs
---------------------------------------------------
Adds a coverage-bucket meta-test that scans the aggregated
conformance oracle source files (`conformance_oracle_ext.rs`,
`conformance_oracle_s55.rs`, `conformance_oracle_s74b.rs`) and
asserts each category has at least the expected number of test
functions. The new `CONFORMANCE_COVERAGE_BUCKETS` table lists the
category labels, the `#[test]` fn-name patterns that count for each,
and the minimum test count — e.g. the `core_sql_and_expressions`
bucket requires at least 100 tests matching any of
`string|math|numeric|case|where|having|order|between|like|glob|printf|datetime|expr|boolean`.

This catches regressions where a refactor silently deletes or
renames a whole category of oracle tests: the meta-test will fail
with a concrete "bucket X is under its minimum of N tests" error
instead of the broken coverage only showing up as a quiet drop in
the oracle pass rate.

crates/fsqlite/tests/compat_tests.rs
------------------------------------
New parity test
`open_with_flags_accepts_common_sqlite_ancillary_flags_like_rusqlite`
that pins the "accept and ignore" behaviour landed in the previous
commit. It opens the same file through both rusqlite and
FrankenSQLite using
`READ_WRITE | CREATE | URI | NO_MUTEX | PRIVATE_CACHE`, creates a
table through rusqlite, then reopens through the compat layer and
verifies the table is visible through `SELECT name FROM sqlite_schema`.
Before the compat-flags change, the FrankenSQLite open call would
fail because of the URI/NO_MUTEX/PRIVATE_CACHE bits; this test
locks in the drop-in compatibility we now claim.

crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/shm.rs
-----------------------------
* Adds a `#[allow(clippy::cast_ptr_alignment)]` to
  `atomic_u64_at(ptr, offset)` because the cast from
  `*mut u8 + offset` to `*mut AtomicU64` is intentionally relying
  on the alignment invariant that callers preserve.
* Adds a SAFETY comment spelling out that invariant:
    - Callers only use this on `MAP_SHARED` mmap regions.
    - `mmap` returns page-aligned base addresses.
    - The existing `assert_aligned_u64_offset()` guard keeps the
      final byte offset both 8-byte aligned and in-bounds.

No behaviour change, just the clippy lint and the reasoning now
lives next to the code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
